creator: Hinton, Richard J. (Richard Josiah), 1830-1901, author

"Entered according to Act of Congress, in the year 1877, by Richard J. Hinton, In the Office of the Librarian of Congress, Washington, D.C."--Title page verso
Plates signed: Lith. Britton, Rey & Co., S.F. Frontispiece illustration captioned "Prescott, capital of Arizona". Most illustrations in text unsigned
Includes folded facsimile of: Mapa correspondiente al diario que formo el P.F. Pedro Font del viage que hizo a Monterey y puerto de San Francisco / P.F. Petrus Font, fecit Tubutana anno 1777

Where and what is Arizona? -- An historical sketch -- Physical and geological features -- The territory's mineral resources -- Mines, mills, and locations -- Over valley and mesa -- The upper Santa Cruz Valley -- The Santa Cruz west to Papagueria -- The Babacomori and east -- The towns of Arizona -- Agricultural resources -- Military posts and telegraph -- Climate -- Fauna and flora of Arizona -- The Indian tribes -- Miscellaneous -- The Spanish explorers and missionaries -- The ancient people of Arizona -- The ancient ruins, their extent and character -- Appendix

title: The hand-book to Arizona: : its resources, history, towns, mines, ruins, and scenery. Amply illustrated. Accompanied with a new map of the territory.
